refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate sub-FRs, each addressing a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating different functional requirements and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating the terrain model with site features relevant to planning and safety
Integrate terrain representation with operational policies and safety plans to highlight hazard zones
Provide an interface for planning and execution functions to access and query the worksite model
selected_dp.selected_dp: Create a semantic map annotating the terrain model with site features relevant to planning and safety

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data with operational policies
Interface between map generation and planning/execution modules
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates terrain representation from policy integration and interface access, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data for modeling
Risk that semantic annotation may require frequent updates as site features change
Potential complexity in maintaining consistency between terrain model and safety plans
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating terrain modeling and semantic annotation into distinct DPs to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for processing and annotating terrain data to support planning and safety functions

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives terrain and sensor data for semantic mapping

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands to initiate mapping and annotation tasks

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts based on annotated terrain features relevant to safety

module_containment_scores.notes: Core processing of semantic mapping fits well here with minimal diffusion.
Vehicle module is focused on mobility and control; semantic mapping would diffuse heavily outside.
Teleoperation may use semantic maps but is not responsible for creating them, causing diffusion.
Safety Services consume semantic maps but do not create them; partial containment but some diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the semantic mapping DP, as it directly processes terrain data and annotates it for planning and safety, minimizing diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related annotations are tightly coupled.
Dependency on accurate telemetry data input; interface reliability is critical.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying the creation of a semantic map that annotates the terrain model with relevant site features, fulfilling the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related annotations are tightly coupled.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is appropriately chosen as the IM boundary, with high containment score (8) and low diffusion score (2), indicating minimal diffusion risk. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, confirming the suitability of this containment.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by confirming that the NGHE Solution module produces the semantic map and that it is accessible to planning and safety functions as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with defined interfaces for telemetry input, tasking commands, and safety alerts output.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface reliability requirements for telemetry data to mitigate dependency risks.
Define coordination mechanisms with Safety Services to manage potential overlap in safety-related annotations and avoid diffusion.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed indicating that the current FR, DP, and IM mapping is sound, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks with Safety Services require clarifications and coordination mechanisms to be explicitly defined. This ensures robust interface reliability and risk mitigation before proceeding to further decomposition.

revise.why: To clarify interface reliability requirements for telemetry data and define coordination mechanisms with Safety Services to manage potential overlap in safety-related annotations and avoid diffusion risks.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate IM boundary with high containment and low diffusion scores.
Verification of the semantic map production and accessibility is feasible as per acceptance criteria.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related annotations are tightly coupled.
Dependency risks due to telemetry data interface reliability.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface reliability requirements for telemetry data.
Defined coordination protocols with Safety Services to prevent diffusion and ensure safety-critical annotations are managed properly.
